item.title: Spark DPP(Dynamic Partition Pruning)
item.author: seilylook
item.description: Partition Pruning in Spark In standard database pruning means that the optimizer will avoid reading files that cannot contain the data that you are looking for. For example, 1 2 3 SELECT * FROM students WHERE subject = 'English'; In this simple query, we are trying to match and identify records in the Students table that belong to subject English. This translates into a simple form that is a filter on top of a scan which means whole data gets scanned first and then filtered out according to the condition.
